alerts.action_required: Monitor BP, ensure adherence to Amlodipine, consider lifestyle modifications.
alerts.description: Patient's BP is 138/88 mmHg, indicating Stage 1 Hypertension, which requires continued monitoring and management.

alerts.action_required: Recommend weight management strategies and dietary counseling.
alerts.description: Patient's BMI of 29 is classified as overweight, which can exacerbate knee osteoarthritis and other comorbidities like diabetes and hypertension.

possible_diagnoses.reasoning: Patient presents with chronic right knee pain, worsening with activity, morning stiffness, mild swelling, crepitus, reduced range of motion, and X-ray findings of joint space narrowing and osteophyte formation, all consistent with osteoarthritis. This is also listed as a provisional diagnosis.

treatment_recommendations.guideline_reference: American College of Rheumatology (ACR) guidelines for OA
treatment_recommendations.suggestion: Consider initiating topical or oral NSAIDs for pain relief, taking into account patient's comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes). Acetaminophen can be considered as a first-line agent for mild to moderate pain.
Recommend physical therapy focusing on strengthening exercises for quadriceps and hamstrings, range of motion exercises, and pain management techniques.

treatment_recommendations.guideline_reference: General medical guidelines for OA, diabetes, and hypertension
treatment_recommendations.suggestion: Advise weight loss to reduce mechanical stress on the knee joint, which can significantly improve OA symptoms and help manage diabetes and hypertension. Refer to a dietitian if appropriate.
Continue current medications for hypertension (Amlodipine), Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (Metformin), and dyslipidemia (Atorvastatin). Ensure regular monitoring of blood glucose and blood pressure.
If conservative measures fail to provide adequate pain relief, consider intra-articular corticosteroid injections for acute pain flares.

lab_values: X-ray right knee shows joint space narrowing and osteophyte formation.
symptoms: Pain in the right knee for the past 3 months, gradually worsening
Dull aching pain in the right knee that increases while walking, climbing stairs, and standing for long periods
Pain is worse in the morning and after prolonged activity
Mild swelling and stiffness lasting about 20 minutes in the morning
